description_long: This is a commercial passenger airplane with a streamlined fuselage, painted primarily white with the Lufthansa logo and branding on the side. The aircraft features a pair of swept-back, low-mounted wings with underwing twin turbofan engines, and a blue vertical stabilizer adorned with the Lufthansa emblem. The nose is rounded and houses a forward, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, while the main cabin windows run along the length of the fuselage. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers extending from either side. The landing gear is in a tricycle configuration, with visible main and nose gear units.
description: A Lufthansa-branded commercial airplane with low-mounted swept wings, twin underwing engines, and a blue vertical stabilizer. The fuselage is white with multiple windows and a forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rounded nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetry of the low-mounted wings and engines.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the Lufthansa branding, blue tail fin, and the full row of passenger windows.
description_view_2: Rear oblique view highlighting the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the overall wing sweep.
description_view_3: Right side view with clear visibility of the Lufthansa logo on the tail and the aircraft's full profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back configuration with no visible winglets and moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and one nose gear unit.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
